questions.question: A motorboat whose speed in still water is $18 \text{ km/h}$ takes $1$ hour more to go $24 \text{ km}$ upstream than to return downstream to the same spot. Let $x \text{ km/h}$ be the speed of the stream. Form a quadratic equation in $x$ and solve it using the factorisation method to find the speed of the stream.

steps: Define the speed of the boat upstream as $(18 - x) \text{ km/h}$ and downstream as $(18 + x) \text{ km/h}$.
Set up the time equation based on the formula $\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}}$: $\frac{24}{18 - x} - \frac{24}{18 + x} = 1$.
Multiply by the common denominator $(18 - x)(18 + x)$ to eliminate fractions: $24(18 + x) - 24(18 - x) = (18 - x)(18 + x)$.
Simplify the equation to standard form: $432 + 24x - 432 + 24x = 324 - x^2$, which leads to $x^2 + 48x - 324 = 0$.
Factorise the quadratic by splitting the middle term: $x^2 + 54x - 6x - 324 = 0$.
Since the speed cannot be negative, conclude that the speed of the stream is $6 \text{ km/h}$.

retrieved_sources: Chapter_3 (Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables).pdf
